name: Grandmother's Southern Fried Okra
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Fresh okra, cut into 1 inch pieces
1 Jalapeno pepper halved & sliced - remove seeds if too hot
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the okra, diced tomato, chopped onion, minced garlic (if using), and sliced jalapeno.
2. In a separate bowl, whisk together the beaten eggs, salt, black pepper, and milk.
3. Pour the egg mixture over the vegetables and toss to thoroughly coat.
4. Gradually add cornmeal until the veggies and the bottom of the bowl are evenly coated. Continue tossing until all ingredients are well mixed. The mixture will have a gooey consistency.
5. Heat the vegetable oil in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat until hot. Test readiness by dropping a small amount of cornmeal into the oil; it should sizzle.
6. Spoon the mixture evenly into the skillet, reducing the heat to medium-low.
7. Cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, or until the underside is golden brown.
8. Invert the coated side onto a plate and slide back into the skillet to cook uncovered for an additional 5-8 minutes until golden brown on both sides.
9. Remove from the skillet and drain excess oil on paper towels before serving hot.
